Headline: DOJ Announces $15M Settlement with Cerebral Over Prescribing Practices
Summary: Department of Justice reached settlement with digital mental health company Cerebral over allegations of improper controlled substance prescribing and inadequate clinical oversight of telehealth providers.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Headway must ensure prescribing oversight protocols exceed standards cited in this enforcement action

Headline: California AG Sues BetterHelp Over Data Sharing with Social Media Platforms
Summary: California Attorney General filed lawsuit alleging BetterHelp shared patient therapy data with Meta and Snapchat for advertising purposes without adequate patient consent.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Reinforces need for strict data governance and marketing pixel audit on Headway platform

Headline: CMS Proposes Mandatory Behavioral Health Quality Reporting for Medicare Advantage Plans
Summary: Proposed rule would require MA plans to report depression screening rates PHQ-9 response rates and 30-day follow-up rates after psychiatric hospitalization starting 2027.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Headway's outcome data capabilities position well for payer quality reporting requirements

Headline: Bipartisan Senate Bill Would Mandate Insurance Coverage of Digital Therapeutics
Summary: CONNECT for Health Act expansion would require commercial insurers to cover FDA-cleared digital therapeutics including mental health applications as a covered benefit.
Relevance_to_Headway: Medium — Could expand reimbursement landscape for digital mental health tools Headway may integrate

Headline: New York Passes Telehealth Parity Permanence Act
Summary: New York legislature passes bill making temporary pandemic telehealth payment parity permanent. Requires commercial insurers to reimburse telehealth visits at the same rate as in-person for behavioral health.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Directly benefits Headway providers and patients in New York; model for other state advocacy

Headline: FTC Issues Warning Letters to 5 Mental Health Apps Over Deceptive Outcome Claims
Summary: FTC sent warning letters to five unnamed digital mental health companies alleging unsubstantiated claims about treatment efficacy and outcome improvement percentages in marketing materials.
Relevance_to_Headway: Medium — Headway should audit all marketing claims about patient outcomes for FTC compliance

Headline: Class Action Filed Against Talkspace Alleging Inadequate Therapist Credentialing
Summary: Former patients filed class action lawsuit alleging Talkspace failed to verify provider credentials and allowed unlicensed individuals to provide therapy through the platform.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Reinforces importance of Headway's credentialing verification processes and documentation

Headline: Texas Medical Board Proposes New Telehealth Prescribing Restrictions
Summary: Texas Medical Board published proposed rules requiring in-person evaluation before prescribing Schedule II controlled substances via telehealth. Would override pandemic-era flexibilities.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Would affect Headway psychiatrists prescribing stimulants for ADHD in Texas

Headline: HHS OCR Settles with Telehealth Provider Over HIPAA Right of Access Violation
Summary: HHS Office for Civil Rights reached $1.2M settlement with unnamed telehealth provider that failed to provide patients with timely access to their health records.
Relevance_to_Headway: Medium — Headway must ensure patient record access requests are fulfilled within HIPAA timelines

Headline: State Telehealth Compact Expansion: PSYPACT Adds 5 New States in 2026
Summary: Interstate psychology practice compact PSYPACT now covers 45 states after adding Montana Vermont New Hampshire Delaware and Hawaii effective January 2026.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Expands Headway psychologist network flexibility; reduces licensure barriers for providers

Headline: FDA Releases Final Guidance on AI/ML-Based Clinical Decision Support in Behavioral Health
Summary: FDA published final guidance clarifying regulatory pathway for AI-powered clinical decision support tools used in mental health settings. Tools meeting certain criteria exempt from device regulation.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Directly relevant to Headway's AI-assisted features; provides clearer regulatory framework

Headline: Illinois Becomes First State to Require AI Transparency in Healthcare Settings
Summary: Illinois Governor signs Healthcare AI Transparency Act requiring providers to disclose when AI is used in clinical decision-making or documentation. Effective July 2026.
Relevance_to_Headway: Medium — Headway will need to update consent flows and disclosures for Illinois patients using AI features

Headline: Ninth Circuit Rules Telehealth Companies Must Comply with State Consumer Protection Laws
Summary: Appeals court ruling establishes that telehealth platforms operating nationally must comply with consumer protection laws in each state where they have patients not just their state of incorporation.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Reinforces need for state-by-state compliance review of all patient-facing communications

Headline: Medicaid Managed Care Plans Face New Mental Health Access Requirements
Summary: CMS finalizes rule requiring Medicaid MCOs to meet specific appointment wait time standards for behavioral health: 10 business days for routine and 48 hours for urgent needs.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Relevant to Headway's Centene Medicaid pilot; must demonstrate appointment availability compliance

Headline: Record $4.75M HIPAA Fine Issued to Mental Health Practice for Unencrypted PHI
Summary: OCR issued largest-ever HIPAA fine against a mental health practice group after breach affecting 120000 patients traced to unencrypted database and lack of risk assessment.
Relevance_to_Headway: Medium — Reinforces criticality of encryption and regular security risk assessments at Headway

Headline: DEA Extends COVID Telehealth Prescribing Flexibilities Through December 2026
Summary: DEA announced extension of temporary rules allowing Schedule II-V controlled substance prescribing via telehealth without initial in-person visit. Permanent rule still under development.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Allows Headway psychiatrists to continue current prescribing workflows; monitor permanent rule

Headline: Oregon Mandates Outcome Reporting for All Behavioral Health Providers
Summary: Oregon Health Authority issues mandate requiring all licensed behavioral health providers to submit standardized outcome measures (PHQ-9 GAD-7) quarterly starting July 2026.
Relevance_to_Headway: High — Headway's MBC infrastructure is well-positioned; opportunity to support provider compliance

Headline: Multi-State AG Investigation into Digital Health Marketing Practices Announced
Summary: Attorneys General from 12 states announced coordinated investigation into marketing practices of digital health companies focusing on unsubstantiated efficacy claims and misleading patient testimonials.
Relevance_to_Headway: Medium — Headway should proactively review all state-specific marketing compliance requirements

Headline: Study Finds Significant Variability in Telehealth Informed Consent Practices
Summary: Research analyzing 42 telehealth platforms found only 31% obtained informed consent that met all recommended elements for telehealth-specific risks and limitations.
Relevance_to_Headway: Medium — Headway should benchmark informed consent against study recommendations and APA guidelines

Headline: EEOC Issues Guidance on Employer Use of AI Mental Health Screening Tools
Summary: EEOC published guidance warning that employer use of AI-powered mental health screening in wellness programs may violate ADA and GINA if not properly implemented.
Relevance_to_Headway: Low — Indirect relevance; relevant if Headway pursues employer wellness channel
